templates.text: Payoff statement from {current_beneficiary} for Deed of Trust recorded as Instrument No. {instrument_number} on {recording_date}, lifecycle identifier {lifecycle_id}. Payoff must reflect a cure through {effective_date}.
templates.why_template: Generated because lifecycle {lifecycle_id} is currently open. Without a payoff, the closing cannot disburse funds to satisfy the lien or obtain a release.

templates.text: Verification of assignment or MERS milestone documentation bridging {originator} to {current_holder} for Instrument No. {instrument_number}. If no recorded assignment exists, a MERS Milestone Report or endorsed note will satisfy.
templates.why_template: Generated from anomaly {anomaly_id} ({anomaly_title}). The release executor differs from the original beneficiary; the examiner must confirm note custody.

templates.text: Certificate of trust or trustee's affidavit for {trust_name}, including full name, date of execution, identity of trustees, and powers to convey.
templates.why_template: Generated from anomaly {anomaly_id} ({anomaly_title}). The grantor of record is a trust entity; trustee authority must be confirmed before closing.

templates.text: HOA estoppel letter and subordination from {hoa_name} if applicable to subdivision {subdivision_name}, confirming no outstanding assessments or violations through {effective_date}.
templates.why_template: Generated because the parcel lies within a recorded subdivision ({subdivision_name}) that typically imposes HOA dues. A lien for unpaid dues would take priority over a new DOT.

templates.text: Tax certificate from {county_treasurer} confirming real property taxes are paid through {effective_date}, or proof of current-year installment payment.
templates.why_template: Required on every residential closing. Unpaid property taxes are a statutory lien senior to the DOT being insured.

templates.text: Curative affidavit executed by {grantor_name} addressing {chain_anomaly_summary}, including confirmation that no outstanding claims of heirship, marriage, or right of occupancy affect the property as of {effective_date}.
templates.why_template: Generated from anomaly {anomaly_id}. The recorded chain contains an ambiguity that is commonly cured via a sworn affidavit rather than requiring a new deed.

templates.text: Record warranty deed from {seller} to {buyer}, conveying fee simple interest in the subject property.
templates.why_template: A {transaction_type} transaction requires a deed transferring ownership. Without a recorded deed, the buyer has no insurable interest.

templates.text: Record new Deed of Trust in favor of {new_lender} securing a {loan_amount} obligation by {borrower}.
templates.why_template: The {transaction_type} transaction creates a new lien that must be recorded to perfect the lender's security interest.

templates.text: Subordination agreement from {junior_lender} (Instrument No. {junior_instrument}) acknowledging priority of the new Deed of Trust. Junior lifecycle: {junior_lifecycle_id}.
templates.why_template: The new DOT must hold {target_position} priority. Without subordination, the existing junior lien ({junior_lifecycle_id}) would remain senior by recording date.

templates.text: Certificate of trust or successor-trustee affidavit for {trust_name}, confirming authority to convey, identity of current trustee(s), and that the trust has not been revoked or amended to remove the property. Trust instrument recording: {trust_instrument_number}.
templates.why_template: Current vesting is in a trust entity. Arizona A.R.S. § 14-11013 allows a certification of trust in lieu of producing the full trust agreement, but the certification must be provided before closing.

templates.text: Letters testamentary, letters of administration, or affidavit of succession for the estate of {decedent_name}. Death certificate recording: {death_cert_instrument}.
templates.why_template: The vested owner is deceased. Authority to convey must be established through probate or small-estate proceedings before title can transfer.

templates.text: Payoff statement from {current_beneficiary} for Deed of Trust recorded as Instrument No. {instrument_number} on {recording_date} (lifecycle {lifecycle_id}). This is the specific DOT being {action} in this {transaction_type} transaction. Payoff must reflect a cure through {effective_date}.
templates.why_template: The examiner selected lifecycle {lifecycle_id} as the DOT being {action}. The closing agent must obtain a payoff demand to disburse funds correctly.

templates.text: Verify that Deed of Trust recorded as Instrument No. {instrument_number} on {recording_date} (lifecycle {lifecycle_id}) remains in first-lien position and has not been modified or subordinated.
templates.why_template: The new {transaction_type} lien is intended as a junior encumbrance. The first-position DOT must be confirmed as undisturbed so that the new lender's subordinate position is clearly defined.

templates.text: Record termination of existing HELOC (Instrument No. {instrument_number}, lifecycle {lifecycle_id}) in favor of {beneficiary}. Obtain zero-balance confirmation from servicer.
templates.why_template: The examiner indicated this existing HELOC should be terminated as part of the {transaction_type} transaction. Without termination, the revolving credit line remains a lien of record.

templates.text: Perform bring-down search from {verified_through} through closing date to confirm no intervening recordings, judgments, or liens.
templates.why_template: Required on every transaction. The last verified search date is {verified_through}; any instrument recorded after that date but before closing would be missed without a bring-down.
